title: Respawn handler — bot.once('spawn') doesn't re-fire on death/respawn
description:
Found during code audit 2026-06-27. The adapter registers `bot.once('spawn')` which only fires on the FIRST spawn. If the bot dies and respawns, no event re-triggers. The C# side receives the `death` event but gets no fresh `spawn` event with updated position/health after respawn.

Impact:
- `spawnPos` is only set once (first spawn) — wander distance-from-spawn constraint uses stale origin after respawn
- C# WorldState doesn't get fresh position on respawn until next GetStatus
- If the bot is teleported away after respawn, WorldState position is stale

Fix:
1. Change `bot.once('spawn')` to `bot.on('spawn')` (or keep both for first-spawn vs respawn distinction)
2. On respawn, reset `spawnPos` to the new position
3. Emit `spawn` event with current position/health/food on each respawn
4. If keeping first-spawn distinction, emit `respawn` event on subsequent spawns

Files:
- MineflayerAdapter/index.js line ~247 (bot.once('spawn'))

Ref: Code audit SteveBot 2026-06-27. See /memories/repo/adapter-event-contracts.md.
